old_claim.note: Classical Historiography (Herodotus, Thucydides) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Humanist education included history in the classical studia humanitatis, and classical historical models shaped civic humanist historiography, so classical historiography is an enabling intellectual model rather than a hard requirement.

source_shape.source_locator: The ideal of humanitas section says the studia humanitatis included history and that classical philosophy, rhetoric, and history were humanist models of proper method.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Classical history was part of humanist study and classical history supplied humanists with methodological models.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge stays enabling because the source supports classical historiography as an intellectual model, not a physical or exclusive prerequisite.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: humanism still has classical historiography as an enabling prerequisite, now with textbook evidence and a scoped note.
would_reject_if: The humanism node is later narrowed to non-historical moral philosophy only.
A stronger source shows history was not part of the early studia humanitatis.
The edge is strengthened to required without evidence that humanism could not form without classical historical writing.
short_reason: Classical history belonged to humanist study and supplied methodological models.
